---
title: "TikZ / Forest Test"
format:
  html:
    include-in-header:
      - text: |
          <!-- TikZJax renders TeX/TikZ directly in the browser -->
          <script src="https://tikzjax.com/v1/tikzjax.js"></script>
  pdf:
    include-in-header:
      - text: |
          \usepackage{tikz}
          \usepackage{forest}
execute:
  echo: false
---

# Plain TeX Test

This should render a simple formula in both HTML and PDF.

```{=html}
<script type="text/tikz">
\node[draw, rounded corners] {$E = mc^2$};
</script>
```

```{=latex}
\begin{tikzpicture}
\node[draw, rounded corners] {$E = mc^2$};
\end{tikzpicture}
```

# Simple TikZ Test

```{=html}
<script type="text/tikz">
\draw (0,0) rectangle (3,1);
\node at (1.5,0.5) {Hello TikZ};
</script>
```

```{=latex}
\begin{tikzpicture}
\draw (0,0) rectangle (3,1);
\node at (1.5,0.5) {Hello TikZ};
\end{tikzpicture}
```

# Forest Syntax Tree Test

```{=html}
<script type="text/tikz">
\usepackage{forest}

\begin{forest}
[IP
  [NP
    [Det [meine]]
    [N′
      [N [Schwester]]
    ]
  ]
  [I
    [VP
      [V′
        [V [schreibt]]
        [PP
          [P [an]]
          [NP
            [Det [einem]]
            [N′
              [AP [wichtigen]]
              [N [Buch]]
            ]
          ]
        ]
      ]
    ]
  ]
]
\end{forest}
</script>
```

```{=latex}
\begin{forest}
[IP
  [NP
    [Det [meine]]
    [N′
      [N [Schwester]]
    ]
  ]
  [I
    [VP
      [V′
        [V [schreibt]]
        [PP
          [P [an]]
          [NP
            [Det [einem]]
            [N′
              [AP [wichtigen]]
              [N [Buch]]
            ]
          ]
        ]
      ]
    ]
  ]
]
\end{forest}
```

# R-Generated Forest Example

```{r}
#| results: asis

tree <- '
\\begin{forest}
[NP
  [Det [the]]
  [N′
    [N [book]]
  ]
]
\\end{forest}
'
test<-"\\draw (0,0) rectangle (3,1);
\\node at (1.5,0.5) {Hello TikZ};"
if (knitr::is_html_output()) {
  cat('<script type="text/tikz">\n')
  cat('\\usepackage{forest}\n')
  cat(tree)
  cat(test)
  cat('\n</script>\n')
} else {
  cat(tree)
}

```
